finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:9 (Call 1: directive — 'Don't,' she said — no verdict); chapters/chapter_14.md:41-45 (Call 2: Simone probes with 'What were you thinking about during the pool story?', Doug answers imperfectly with false start, Simone closes with logistics 'Call me after Detroit' — no diagnostic verdict); chapters/chapter_14.md:125-153 (Call 3: Simone withholds, Doug does inferential work at line 143, Simone confirms with 'Yeah' at line 145 and does not tell him what to do — verdict absent by design); chapters/chapter_14.md:83 (false start restored from snapshot: 'That's not —' preserves productive roughness smoothed away in prior pass)
finding_results.notes: The three Simone calls now have three distinct shapes: (1) embedded directive, (2) probe that gathers data without assessing it, (3) deliberate withholding that forces Doug to do the inferential work. No two calls end on a report-then-diagnostic-verdict ladder. No call closes on a neat standalone sentence summarizing the chapter's theme. Additionally restored a false start in the heckler scene (line 83) where the prior pass had smoothed Doug's 'That's not —' into cleaner phrasing, per snapshot-anchor instructions.

summary: Single finding addressed. Call 2 (post-Pittsburgh) revised to replace Simone's diagnostic verdict ('That's useful') with a probing question she does not answer for Doug, ending on logistics instead of assessment. This differentiates it from Call 1 (directive) and Call 3 (withholding). A false start in the heckler scene was restored from the snapshot to preserve productive spoken roughness. Total diff: +4 lines in Call 2, 1 line modified in heckler exchange. Chapter heading contract preserved. No sanitization, no literary polish introduced.
